title: Introduction to ARM Assembly
summary: Understand what assembly language is, the role of the assembler, and the ARM processor family.
content_html:
Assembly language is a low-level programming language that uses human-readable mnemonics (like MOV, ADD) to represent machine instructions directly.

Abstraction Layers

High-level (Python/C) → Compiler → Assembly → Assembler → Machine code (binary)

An assembler (like FASMARM) translates assembly into binary machine code. A compiler translates high-level code to assembly first.

ARM Architecture

ARM (Advanced RISC Machine) is a RISC (Reduced Instruction Set Computer) architecture used in most mobile devices (phones, tablets) and the Raspberry Pi.

  • Simple, fixed-width 32-bit instructions
  • 16 general-purpose registers (R0–R15)
  • Load/Store architecture: memory only accessed by LDR/STR
  • High performance per watt — ideal for mobile

Raspberry Pi and ARM

The Raspberry Pi uses an ARM Cortex processor running at 700MHz–1.8GHz. We write bare metal assembly directly — code that runs without an OS.

Tools in COS10004

  • FASMARM: ARM assembler (Windows and Linux)
  • Raspberry Pi: the physical computer we program
